description_long: This object is a traditional hand-cranked grinding wheel mounted on a sturdy wooden frame. The main feature is a large, circular stone wheel with a rough, gray surface, designed for sharpening tools. The wheel is supported by a rectangular wooden stand with four legs, reinforced by crossbars and metal bolts at the joints for stability. A metal crank handle is attached to the side of the wheel, allowing manual rotation. The frame is made of dark-stained wood, contrasting with the lighter, textured stone. This type of grinding wheel is commonly found in workshops, garages, or outdoor urban settings for tool maintenance.
description: A hand-cranked grinding wheel with a large stone disc mounted on a robust wooden frame, featuring a metal crank handle for manual operation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full circular stone wheel and the wooden frame with visible bolts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the stone wheel and the protruding metal crank handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the stone wheel and the wooden frame structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the stone wheel's edge and the crank handle's attachment.
